name: Registry & Methodology
rationale: The crediting program and specific methodology determine the rules and rigor under which credits are generated. v0.6 simplifies from 4 tiers + 4 modifiers (inter-rater κ=0.168, weakest dimension) to 2 tiers + 2 Boolean modifiers to improve inter-rater reliability.
data_source: Token metadata -- registry ID + methodology ID (on-chain native via mapping table)
v0_6_change: Collapsed from 4 tiers (70-100 / 40-69 / 10-39 / 0-9) with 4 prose-based modifiers to 2 tiers with 2 Boolean modifiers. Motivation: LLM panel IRR study (data/llm-panel-irr/) found registry_methodology had the worst Fleiss' κ (0.168, 'slight' agreement). The 4-tier × 4-modifier combinatorial space (1920 possible outcomes) produced too much assessor variance. The new 2-tier × 2-modifier structure has ~40 possible outcomes. Target: κ > 0.40 on re-run.

tiers.condition: Program is ICVCM CCP-Eligible AND methodology is CCP-approved (per icvcm.org/ccp-approved-methodologies/)
tiers.guidance: Score 75 for a CCP methodology with known limitations or conditions (e.g., cookstoves with constrained fNRB); 80 for standard CCP methodology; 85 for CCP methodology with peer-reviewed conservative baseline.
examples: Gold Standard TPDDTEC (cookstoves, CCP with conditions)

tiers.condition: Program is NOT CCP-Eligible, OR methodology is not CCP-approved, OR no registry
tiers.guidance: Score 45-50 for an ICVCM-assessed program (Verra/GS/ACR/CAR) with a non-CCP methodology that has no known integrity concerns; 35-40 for an emerging registry (Plan Vivo, Regen, OFP, Nori); 25-30 for unrecognized registry, ICVCM-rejected methodology (grid RE, HFC-23, waste heat recovery), or no registry.

modifiers.condition: Published independent methodology review confirms conservative crediting baseline (e.g., Calyx peer-reviewed assessment, ICVCM assessment report, or academic analysis)
Published peer-reviewed research demonstrates systematic overcrediting for this specific methodology (e.g., West et al. 2023 for VM0009 REDD+; Nature Communications 2024 for grid wind)

scoring_procedure: 1. Determine tier (CCP or Non-CCP) based on ICVCM public list. 2. Pick base score within the tier band per guidance. 3. Apply Boolean modifiers if evidence exists. 4. Clamp to [0, 100].
notes: The CCP-eligible determination is binary and references icvcm.org/ccp-approved-methodologies/ — the same source the Solidity contract would query via an oracle or mapping table.
Removed the old 'under_review' modifier because it was subjective; replaced by the 'known_overcrediting' modifier which requires published evidence.
The ICVCM-rejected category (grid RE, HFC-23, waste heat recovery) maps to the bottom of the Non-CCP band (25-30) because ICVCM explicitly evaluated and rejected these methodologies.
CCP-Eligible programs as of Q1 2026: Verra, Gold Standard, ACR, CAR, ART, Puro.earth, Isometric, Equitable Earth, Rainbow Standard.
